meta.purpose: Config for the Presentation tab. For each eligible event, tells the UI which of the three input tools (Script / Audio / Files) to load and what slice of the official rating sheet each tool scores. Derived from presentation_rubrics.json (verified) + the input-requirements planning doc.

tools.script: Typed/pasted text (speech, report text, or plan). Scores the CONTENT + FORMAT/SOURCES lines.
tools.audio: Voice recording. Coaches the DELIVERY lines (pace, fillers, projection). NOTE: audio catches vocal delivery only; eye contact/body language need video, so delivery credit from audio is partial.
tools.files: Uploaded artifact (report PDF, slide deck, resume). For document events the file IS the graded deliverable; for presentation-only events it's a supporting deck.

meta.ui_rule: Load every tool marked default:true. Tools marked default:false are offered as optional. When two tools can both feed the content grade (upload a doc OR paste the script), let the student choose in the moment - they score the same rubric lines.

tools.scores: Provides the graded document; scores content+format lines (280 pts)
Paste the same text instead of uploading; scores the same 280 pts
Delivery coaching (10 pts on the sheet; partial from audio - full delivery needs video)
Their speech/plan/analysis script; scores content+format lines (130 pts)
Optional slide deck; supplements the content grade
Delivery coaching (10 pts on the sheet; partial from audio - full delivery needs video)
Their speech/plan/analysis script; scores content+format lines (120 pts)
Optional slide deck; supplements the content grade
Delivery coaching (10 pts on the sheet; partial from audio - full delivery needs video)

events.notes: The captured line items sum to 140, but the official sheet prints a 150 total (one item's max is likely 10 higher than captured). grand_total is set to the honest computed 140; verify the missing 10 against the official PDF before locking.

events.notes: Grand total 180 covers report + presentation only; the objective test scores/advances separately. Vye already handles objective tests, so this event pairs its existing engine with the new grader.

events.notes: Only the 50-pt prejudged assets are auto-gradable from an upload. The 120-pt interview is live; build it as a mock-interview practice mode.
